frameworks.name: perceptual decision-making
frameworks.one_line: challenging visual motion decisions with monetary rewards probe evoked fMRI responses

frameworks.name: reward expectation
frameworks.one_line: dopaminergic midbrain responses interpreted via reward expectation for easier decisions

core_question: Does 7 T fMRI provide a region-specific sensitivity benefit over 3 T for perceptual decision-making responses in cortex, striatum, and brainstem?
key_assumptions: evoked fMRI amplitudes index task-related neural activity
same participants and same task at both field strengths control inter-subject and task variance
decision and post-feedback intervals are separable epochs of interest
manipulations: magnetic field strength (3 T vs 7 T)
key_findings: 7 vs 3 T benefit during decision interval differed across cortical, striatal, and brainstem regions
generally larger 7 vs 3 T benefit in subcortical structures
stronger 7 T responses for easier than harder decisions in dopaminergic midbrain nuclei
interpretations: ultra-high field gain is region-specific, with subcortical structures benefiting most
midbrain easy-versus-hard modulation is consistent with reward expectation signals
